‚ÄôAń©n-Sń©n-Q√£f .
And so you ňĻO Prophetňļ are sent revelation, just like those before you, by Allah‚ÄĒthe Almighty, All-Wise.
To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And He is the Most High, the Greatest.

The heavens nearly burst, one above the other, ňĻin awe of Himňļ. And the angels glorify the praises of their Lord, and seek forgiveness for those on earth. Surely Allah alone is the All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.
As for those who take other protectors besides Him, Allah is Watchful over them. And you ňĻO Prophetňļ are not a keeper over them.

And so We have revealed to you a Quran in Arabic, so you may warn the Mother of Cities <sup foot_note=992>992</sup> and everyone around it, and warn of the Day of Gathering‚ÄĒabout which there is no doubt‚ÄĒňĻwhenňļ a group will be in Paradise and another in the Blaze.

foot_notes.992: ‚ÄúThe Mother of Cities‚ÄĚ is an honorary title given to the city of Mecca because of its great religious significance as the home of Allah‚Äôs first house of worship ever built on earth, and perhaps because of its central location as well.

text: Had Allah willed, He could have easily made all ňĻhumanityňļ into a single community ňĻof believersňļ. But He admits into His mercy whoever He wills. And the wrongdoers will have no protector or helper.
How can they take protectors besides Him? Allah alone is the Protector. He ňĻaloneňļ gives life to the dead. And He ňĻaloneňļ is Most Capable of everything.
ňĻSay to the believers, O Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúWhatever you may differ about, its judgment rests with Allah. That is Allah‚ÄĒmy Lord. In Him I put my trust, and to Him I ňĻalwaysňļ turn.‚ÄĚ

ňĻHe isňļ the Originator of the heavens and the earth. He has made for you spouses from among yourselves, and ňĻmadeňļ mates for cattle ňĻas wellňļ‚ÄĒmultiplying you ňĻbothňļ. There is nothing like Him, for He ňĻaloneňļ is the All-Hearing, All-Seeing.
To Him belong the keys ňĻof the treasuriesňļ of the heavens and the earth. He gives abundant or limited provisions to whoever He wills. Indeed, He has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of all things.

He has ordained for you ňĻbelieversňļ the Way which He decreed for Noah, and what We have revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ and what We decreed for Abraham, Moses, and Jesus, <sup foot_note=994>994</sup> ňĻcommanding:ňļ ‚ÄúUphold the faith, and make no divisions in it.‚ÄĚ What you call the polytheists to is unbearable for them. Allah chooses for Himself whoever He wills, and guides to Himself whoever turns ňĻto Himňļ.

foot_notes.994: Like 33:7, this verse names the five greatest prophets of Islam: Noah, Abraham, Moses, Jesus, and MuŠł•ammad.

text: They did not split ňĻinto sectsňļ out of mutual envy until knowledge came to them. <sup foot_note=995>995</sup> Had it not been for a prior decree from your Lord for an appointed term, <sup foot_note=996>996</sup> the matter would have certainly been settled between them ňĻat onceňļ. And surely those who were made to inherit the Scripture <sup foot_note=997>997</sup> after them are truly in alarming doubt about this ňĻQuranňļ.

foot_notes.995: No community split into believers and disbelievers until they received the knowledge given by their prophet.
foot_notes.996: That He will delay their judgment until the Hereafter.

text: Because of that, you ňĻO Prophetňļ will invite ňĻallňļ. Be steadfast as you are commanded, and do not follow their desires. And say, ‚ÄúI believe in every Scripture Allah has revealed. And I am commanded to judge fairly among you. Allah is our Lord and your Lord. We will be accountable for our deeds and you for yours. There is no ňĻneed forňļ contention between us. Allah will gather us together ňĻfor judgmentňļ. And to Him is the final return.‚ÄĚ

As for those who dispute about Allah after He is ňĻalreadyňļ acknowledged ňĻby manyňļ, their argument is futile in the sight of their Lord. Upon them is wrath, and they will suffer a severe punishment.
It is Allah Who has revealed the Book with the truth and the balance ňĻof justiceňļ. You never know, perhaps the Hour is near.
Those who disbelieve in it ňĻask toňļ hasten it ňĻmockinglyňļ. But the believers are fearful of it, knowing that it is the truth. Surely those who dispute about the Hour have gone far astray.
Allah is Ever Kind to His servants. He provides ňĻabundantlyňļ to whoever He wills. And He is the All-Powerful, Almighty.

Whoever desires the harvest of the Hereafter, We will increase their harvest. And whoever desires ňĻonlyňļ the harvest of this world, We will give them some of it, but they will have no share in the Hereafter.

Or do they have associate-gods who have ordained for them some ňĻpolytheisticňļ beliefs, which Allah has not authorized? Had it not been for ňĻpriorňļ decree on Judgment, the matter would have certainly been settled between them ňĻat onceňļ. And surely the wrongdoers will suffer a painful punishment.

You will see the wrongdoers fearful ňĻof the punishmentňļ for what they committed but it will be inevitable for them, whereas those who believe and do good will be in the lush Gardens of Paradise. They will have whatever they desire from their Lord. That is ňĻtrulyňļ the greatest bounty.

That ňĻrewardňļ is the good news which Allah gives to His servants who believe and do good. Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI do not ask you ňĻMeccansňļ for a reward for this ňĻmessageňļ‚ÄĒonly honour for ňĻourňļ kinship.‚ÄĚ <sup foot_note=998>998</sup> Whoever earns a good deed, We will increase it in goodness for them. Surely Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Appreciative.

foot_notes.998: This could also be a call to all believers to love and respect the immediate family of the Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) .

text: Or do they say, ‚ÄúHe has fabricated a lie about Allah!‚ÄĚ? ňĻIf you had,ňļ Allah would have sealed your heart, if He willed. And Allah wipes out falsehood and establishes the truth by His Words. He certainly knows best what is ňĻhiddenňļ in the heart.
He is the One Who accepts repentance from His servants and pardons ňĻtheirňļ sins. And He knows whatever you do.
He responds to those who believe and do good, and increases their reward out of His grace. As for the disbelievers, they will suffer a severe punishment.

Had Allah given abundant provisions to ňĻallňļ His servants, they would have certainly transgressed throughout the land. But He sends down whatever He wills in perfect measure. He is truly All-Aware, All-Seeing of His servants.
He is the One Who sends down rain after people have given up hope, spreading out His mercy. He is the Guardian, the Praiseworthy.
And among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth, and all living beings He dispersed throughout both. And He is Most Capable of bringing all together whenever He wills.
Whatever affliction befalls you is because of what your own hands have committed. And He pardons much.
You can never escape ňĻHimňļ on earth, nor do you have any protector or helper besides Allah.
And among His signs are the ships like mountains ňĻsailingňļ in the sea.
If He wills, He can calm the wind, leaving the ships motionless on the water. Surely in this are signs for whoever is steadfast, grateful.
Or He can wreck the ships for what the people have committed‚ÄĒthough He forgives much‚ÄĒ
so those who dispute about Our signs may know that they have no refuge.

Whatever ňĻpleasureňļ you have been given is ňĻno more than a fleetingňļ enjoyment of this worldly life. But what is with Allah is far better and more lasting for those who believe and put their trust in their Lord;
who avoid major sins and shameful deeds, and forgive when angered;
who respond to their Lord, establish prayer, conduct their affairs by mutual consultation, and donate from what We have provided for them;
and who enforce justice when wronged.
The reward of an evil deed is its equivalent. But whoever pardons and seeks reconciliation, then their reward is with Allah. He certainly does not like the wrongdoers.
There is no blame on those who enforce justice after being wronged.
Blame is only on those who wrong people and transgress in the land unjustly. It is they who will suffer a painful punishment.
And whoever endures patiently and forgives‚ÄĒsurely this is a resolve to aspire to.
And whoever Allah leaves to stray will have no guide after Him. You will see the wrongdoers, when they face the torment, pleading, ‚ÄúIs there any way back ňĻto the worldňļ?‚ÄĚ

And you will see them exposed to the Fire, fully humbled out of disgrace, stealing glances ňĻat itňļ. And the believers will say, ‚ÄúThe ňĻtrueňļ losers are those who have lost themselves and their families on Judgment Day.‚ÄĚ The wrongdoers will certainly be in everlasting torment.
They will have no protectors to help them against Allah. And whoever Allah leaves to stray, for them there is no way.
Respond to your Lord before the coming of a Day from Allah that cannot be averted. There will be no refuge for you then, nor ňĻgrounds forňļ denial ňĻof sinsňļ. <sup foot_note=999>999</sup>

foot_notes.999: Other possible translations: 1. ‚ÄúYou will have no refuge then, nor will you have an advocate ňĻagainst punishmentňļ.‚ÄĚ 2. ‚ÄúYou will have no refuge then, nor ňĻgroundsňļ to protest ňĻthe tormentňļ.‚ÄĚ

text: But if they turn away, We have not sent you ňĻO Prophetňļ as a keeper over them. Your duty is only to deliver ňĻthe messageňļ. And indeed, when We let someone taste a mercy from Us, they become prideful ňĻbecauseňļ of it. But when afflicted with evil because of what their hands have done, then one becomes totally ungrateful. <sup foot_note=1000>1000</sup>
To Allah ňĻaloneňļ belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He creates whatever He wills. He blesses whoever He wills with daughters, and blesses whoever He wills with sons,
or grants both, sons and daughters, ňĻto whoever He willsňļ, and leaves whoever He wills infertile. He is indeed All-Knowing, Most Capable.

It is not ňĻpossibleňļ for a human being to have Allah communicate with them, except through inspiration, or from behind a veil, or by sending a messenger-angel to reveal whatever He wills by His permission. He is surely Most High, All-Wise.

And so We have sent to you ňĻO Prophetňļ a revelation by Our command. You did not know of ňĻthisňļ Book and faith ňĻbeforeňļ. But We have made it a light, by which We guide whoever We will of Our servants. And you are truly leading ňĻallňļ to the Straight Path‚ÄĒ
the Path of Allah, to Whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Surely to Allah all matters will return ňĻfor judgmentňļ.
